title: FIFA WC semis cheatsheet: Can England stop Messi, will Spain vanquish France?
content: France, Argentina, Spain and England are the last four standing. Here’s the not-so-brief guide to the World Cup’s first semi-final line-up featuring the top four ranked teams.

title: Ahead of England ODIs, captain Gill reveals 2027 WC plans for Kohli, Rohit
content: India captain Shubman Gill reaffirmed that Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma remain central to the ODI team's 2027 World Cup plans, calling them the “backbone” of India’s batting. Emphasising their experience in pressure situations, Gill said India’s settled core will stay intact while younger players are gradually integrated through opportunities in upcoming ODI series.

title: Volkswagen CEO's leaked memo confirms: Up to 100,000 jobs may go at Europe's biggest carmaker
content: Volkswagen CEO Oliver Blume has confirmed in an internal memo to employees that Europe's biggest car maker may cut up to 100,000 jobs, flagging 50,000 more layoffs on top of the 50,000 already agreed. Blume cited a 20% cost disadvantage against rivals and said VW cannot confirm competitive use cases for its Emden, Hanover, Zwickau and Neckarsulm plants. The memo follows worker protests across 18 German sites.

title: Pakistan faces shortage of 100 life-saving medicines like cancer drugs, morphine
content: Pakistan faces a critical shortage of over 100 essential medicines, including life-saving drugs.  Delayed price revisions by the Drug Regulatory Authority of Pakistan are disrupting production.  Rising costs of raw materials and currency depreciation have impacted manufacturing expenses significantly.  This situation is creating space for counterfeit and substandard products in the market.  Industry representatives urge the government to approve pending hardship pricing cases immediately.

title: Who guards Hormuz and who gets paid? Trump and Iran clash over control of the strait
content: The conflict between Iran and the United States over the Strait of Hormuz continues. Both nations are contesting control of the vital waterway amid ongoing attacks. The United States conducted significant strikes on Iranian targets following an attack on a container ship. Iran stated it alone has authority to control the Strait of Hormuz. Commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has increasingly shifted out of public view.

title: Watch: Iran fires missile towards US targets with 'GAME OVER USA' painted on it
content: Iran launched a ballistic missile with "GAME OVER USA" painted on its body. This action followed recent United States strikes on Iranian military targets. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps claimed missile and drone operations against American facilities. These strikes targeted bases in Jordan, Bahrain, and Kuwait. Iran warned against further foreign military interference in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Jeff Bezos' ex-wife MacKenzie Scott donates to US youth mental health non-profit
content: MacKenzie Scott has donated twenty million dollars to Active Minds. This significant contribution is the largest in the nonprofit's history. Active Minds focuses on youth mental health awareness and reducing stigma. The unrestricted funds will expand programs and support young leaders. This donation occurs amid growing concerns about youth mental health.

title: Telangana POCSO accused, wanted for slaughtering 6, found dead after 60-hour manhunt
content: In a tragic turn of events, an accused in a POCSO case was discovered deceased following a shocking series of murders. He reportedly took the lives of six individuals, including three innocent children and members of his own family. This individual had a disturbing history, having previously murdered the mother and grandmother of his victim. After abducting a teenager, he ended her life near a lakeside.

title: Hubble shows remains of 1054 supernova still expanding after 1,000 years
content: Astronomers used the Hubble Space Telescope to measure the expanding Crab Nebula.  New images compared with older ones show gas filaments moving outwards.  This ancient stellar explosion's remnant is still actively changing today.  The Crab Pulsar at the center continues to power the nebula's glow.  Scientists also identified previously unrecognised filament structures within the nebula.

title: Why Dubai's housing market slowed in 2026; sales fell 16%, prices dipped by 7%
content: Dubai's residential property sales saw a sixteen percent decrease in early 2026. This slowdown occurred as regional geopolitical tensions impacted buyer sentiment and demand. Despite this, sales remained fifteen percent higher than in the first half of 2024. Residential property prices saw a slight decline between February and April. However, 2025 was a record year for Dubai's property market.

title: Satya Nadella warns AI users: You are paying for your own IP, suggests 5 solutions
content: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella says enterprises using AI are paying twice—once in cash, once in proprietary knowledge. In a post viewed 5.7 million times, he calls it the "Reverse Information Paradox": every prompt, eval and correction leaks institutional know-how to model providers. He flags the irony of fair use for vendors, restrictive terms for buyers, and offers a five-point fix: Control, Capability, Choice, Cost, Compound.

title: A US inmate demanded pasta in Tihar: What prison meal looks like in different countries
content: A US citizen arrested in a terror case seeks court permission to cook his own food. He has been on a hunger strike for over 50 days due to prison food. Matthew Aaron VanDyke claims jail meals are unsuitable for his dietary habits. His family is willing to cover all expenses for his personal cooking setup. The court has sought a response from Tihar Jail authorities.

title: Youth thrashed by mob while with woman of another faith in UP
content: A youth was allegedly assaulted by a mob in Muzaffarnagar after being found with a woman of a different faith. A video of the incident surfaced online, prompting an immediate police investigation into the matter. Police reached the spot and rescued both individuals from the agitated crowd. Officials are currently verifying identities and investigating the circumstances surrounding the confrontation.

title: Canucks explore trade options for former superstar Elias Pettersson
content: Elias Pettersson surrounded by NHL trade rumors as Vancouver Canucks look ahead to the future. His inconsistent play has made his trade value, as the former franchise centerpiece, suspect throughout the league. Vancouver will now consider potential offers, contract issues and whether trading Pettersson could accelerate a roster shake-up this offseason.

title: Sid Seixeiro questions Auston Matthews' leadership after Marner comments
content: Craig Berube unveiled Mitch Marner’s significant emotional presence in the locker room, and Sid Seixeiro has started a conversation on the leadership make-up of the Toronto Maple Leafs. The comments have raised questions about Auston Matthews’ captaincy, team culture and the impact of losing Marner. The discussion comes after a disappointing 2025-26 season in Toronto without the longtime forward.

title: Jude Bellingham credits his parents for shaping his football journey
content: Denise and Mark Bellingham have been central to Jude Bellingham’s rise from a young footballer in Birmingham to one of England’s biggest stars. Through career sacrifices, constant support, and important decisions, they have helped shape both Jude and Jobe’s careers. While reports have questioned their relationship, they continue to stand behind their sons’ success.

title: IND vs ENG 1st ODI: Joe Root returns as England announce playing XI - See full team
content: Jacob Bethell will open batting with Ben Duckett for England's first ODI. Jos Buttler plays his 200th ODI match, marking a significant career milestone. England's bowling attack features a spin-heavy approach against India's strong batting lineup. Jofra Archer returns to the ODI squad after a strong T20I performance. The three-match series begins at Edgbaston on Tuesday.

title: 'Get ready for sudden death': Iran media's Lindsey reference in warning to Trump, Bibi
content: Iranian newspapers featured Senator Lindsey Graham's death on their front pages. Some publications issued veiled warnings directed at the US and its leaders. One newspaper described Graham as the "Merchant of Death" following his sudden illness. State media celebrated his passing, calling him "sent to hell." These remarks fueled speculation among some supporters of President Trump's movement.

title: Anupam Kher reacts to 6-year old video of Naseeruddin, responds to trolling over Ram Mandir comments
content: Anupam Kher addressed an old video of Naseeruddin Shah calling him a clown. He stated that he and Naseeruddin Shah respect each other and have met recently. Kher emphasized his achievements are due to hard work and divine grace. He believes trolls are trying to target him after his Ram Janmbhoomi comments. Kher concluded by asserting that self-made individuals are not afraid of abuse.

title: 'Why the axe is always on Sanju?': Ex-chief selector blasts Gambhir-led management
content: MSK Prasad advised patience regarding coach Gautam Gambhir's early tenure.  He noted key players Hardik Pandya and Jasprit Bumrah were absent during recent losses.  Prasad stated assessing Gambhir requires a full-strength team for future matches.  He also criticized the decision to drop Sanju Samson after a few low scores.  The former selector also expressed concern over Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's gradual introduction.

title: Days after love marriage, Bengaluru woman dies by suicide after husband 'delays' visit
content: A tragic incident near Bengaluru has left a newly-married woman dead after she ingested poison. Authorities believe she may have taken her own life following a conversation with her husband, Ravindra, who had eloped with her after a whirlwind romance. His inability to be with her right away seems to have caused her emotional turmoil. Investigations are underway to uncover the full details surrounding her demise.

title: India's 40-year-old streak in England continues with historic Lord's win
content: India’s historic 270-run win over England in the first women’s Test at Lord’s extended their unbeaten Test record in England to 40 years. Yastika Bhatia’s landmark century and Kranti Gaud’s seven-wicket match haul powered the victory. India now lead the overall head-to-head 4-1, with 11 draws, underlining their growing dominance in women’s Test cricket.

title: Watch: Iran 'completely destroys' Patriot air defences, fuel tanks, radar at US Gulf bases
content: Iran's Revolutionary Guard claimed missile and drone strikes on US bases in three countries. These operations targeted air defense systems and fuel depots in retaliation. The IRGC stated the attacks were a response to recent American military strikes. US Central Command confirmed precision strikes against Iranian military installations. Both sides engaged in escalating military actions in the region.

title: 9 deaths in 6 days, 18 since May: A wave of maternal deaths in Rajasthan
content: Rajasthan's public healthcare system is under scrutiny after nine women died in government hospitals. Expert teams are investigating these recent maternal fatalities in Bhilwara and Banswara.  Previous deaths in other districts have also raised serious concerns about patient safety.  Mumbai faces challenges with high-risk referral cases, while Delhi sees rising maternal mortality.  Grieving families seek answers, accountability, and reforms to prevent future tragedies.

title: Teen dies as SUV driven by minor hits scooter, crashes into school bus
content: A 17-year-old boy died after a speeding car hit his scooter. The car then crashed into a school mini-bus on Warehouse Road. The teenager was critically injured and later succumbed to his injuries. Eyewitnesses reported the car driver and occupants fled the scene. Police are searching for the absconding driver and scanning CCTV footage.

title: ‘To be signed at right time’: India-US trade deal framework ‘ready’, says official
content: India-US trade deal: India is seeking a tariff structure under the proposed agreement that provides it with a comparative advantage over competing countries.

title: Watch: Puja inside moving train coach stuns social media; Railway replies
content: A viral video showed a priest performing rituals inside a moving train coach. Northern Railway clarified the ceremony occurred in a privately booked saloon car. This luxury coach was commercially booked through IRCTC for a specific journey. The railway stated no passengers were inconvenienced by the private booking. The incident followed another video of a decorated train coach causing outrage.

title: India's imports outpace exports, trade deficit expands to $30.4 billion
content: India's merchandise imports surged thirty-one percent year-on-year in June. Exports also rose fifteen point five percent, while imports climbed sharply. The country's trade deficit stood at thirty point four three billion dollars for the month. During the April-June quarter, exports increased fifteen point nine two percent. Imports during the same period rose nineteen point eight nine percent, widening the deficit.

title: Emotional after Datia ticket snub, Narottam Mishra chokes up at BJP rally
content: Senior BJP leader Narottam Mishra campaigned emotionally for Ashutosh Tiwari's nomination. He pledged to personally campaign in every village for the upcoming by-election. Mishra defended the BJP government's development record and attacked the Congress party. He asserted there was no internal discord within the BJP regarding the candidate. Ashutosh Tiwari thanked the leadership and expressed confidence in meeting expectations.

title: Govinda announces silver-screen comeback with 'Roopa' and 'Duniyadari'
content: Actor Govinda is returning to the big screen with upcoming films 'Roopa' and 'Duniyadari'. He will be seen opposite newcomer Rani Swarnkar in these projects. Govinda is also producing these films, which are aimed at young audiences. He spoke about his personal and professional life at the press conference. The actor was last seen in the 2019 film 'Rangeela Raja'.

title: Uddhav extends support to CJP protest as Wangchuk's hunger strike enters day 16
content: Uddhav Thackeray has voiced support for the Cockroach Janta Party's protest. He backs climate activist Sonam Wangchuk and CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke's demands. Thackeray urged political parties and citizens to unite for the youth's future. He criticized the government's insensitivity to genuine concerns raised. Thackeray's party MPs will also raise the NEET issue in Parliament.

title: 2020 Delhi riots: Court convicts Tahir Hussain in IB officer Ankit Sharma murder case
content: A Delhi court convicted former AAP councillor Tahir Hussain and others for murder. The court found Hussain guilty of murder and rioting charges related to the 2020 riots. Nazim, Kashim, Anas, and Javed were also convicted for murder and other offenses. The court acquitted six other individuals accused in the case. Arguments on the quantum of sentence will be heard later.

title: Woman thrashed with sticks in front of police, dies
content: In a shocking incident in Chhatarpur, a 55-year-old woman lost her life following a savage attack by several women, allegedly stemming from an old rivalry linked to a romantic affair. Disturbingly, a viral clip reveals the attackers taking breaks for water amid their prolonged assault. The local police are delving into the case, facing criticism for perceived inaction from a Dial-112 response team.

title: Yastika, Kranti inspire India's historic Lord's Test win over England
content: India registered a historic 270-run victory over England in the first-ever women’s Test at Lord’s. Yastika Bhatia’s landmark 113, Richa Ghosh’s unbeaten fifty and Smriti Mandhana’s 70 helped set a daunting 457-run target. Kranti Gaud’s seven-wicket match haul and disciplined bowling from Sneh Rana and Sayali Satghare ensured India sealed a commanding, all-round triumph.

title: NHL Trade Rumors: Penguins linked to $92.8M Western Conference forward
content: Elias Pettersson's trade speculation is gaining real traction after Elliotte Friedman named the Pittsburgh Penguins as a potential destination on his 32 Thoughts podcast. With Vancouver finishing last in the West and a new leadership group publicly pressing Pettersson to raise his standards, the Canucks forward's future is far from settled. His $11.6 million cap hit and player control make any move complicated, but with Pittsburgh seeking one more competitive push alongside Crosby and Malkin, the fit has at least caught the attention of one of the sport's most plugged-in insiders.

title: Drone vs drone: How interceptors are changing the cost equation of air defence
content: Countries are investing in drone countermeasures due to their battlefield prevalence. Ukraine developed interceptor drones to counter cheaper hostile systems effectively. These interceptor drones offer flexibility and can be reused after missions. India is also developing similar drone interceptor systems for defense. These new systems provide a cost-effective solution against drone threats.

title: SC allows CBI, ED to lead Reliance Group probe, says it cannot decide the course of investigation
content: Any comment made by the Supreme Court during an ongoing investigation, even though unintentional, would impact the trial, Chief Justice Surya Kant says; court allows investigative agencies to file their status reports

title: Naidu reviews Polavaram works, stresses timely completion
content: Chief Minister directs officials to complete remaining ECRF dam works, expedite rehabilitation and infrastructure, and reviews progress of 36 priority irrigation projects across Andhra Pradesh

title: Supreme Court rejects claims of over 350 teachers of West Bengal madrasas for state salary
content: The case came up before the apex court with questions, including whether the madrasas in which the petitioners were appointed were validly recognised

title: Development works worth ₹500 crore underway in Kondepi, says Minister
content: Inaugurating a road project at Singarayakonda, Dola Sree Bala Veeranjaneya Swamy criticises the previous government for halting BC residential school project in Singarayakonda, adding that ₹45 crore has been sanctioned to it by the BC Welfare Minister

title: Assam Rifles jawan killed in Nagaland IED blast
content: The incident, which injured four others, occurred when a convoy of vehicles was returning from Sukhovi to Dimapur town

title: Residents flag dangerous road conditions across East Bengaluru’s IT corridor
content: These roads serve major residential areas, IT corridor, schools and hospitals but are filled with potholes and pose a risk to motorists

title: Decision on Cauvery water release after July 15 meeting: Karnataka Chief Minister
content: Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar said the government needs to balance the interests of Karnataka’s farmers, ensure drinking water supply, and comply with court directions while taking a decision on the release of Cauvery water to Tamil Nadu

title: DMK to approach Supreme Court if Governor fails to act on complaints of horse trading by TVK
content: Speaking to reporters after submitting a petition to the Governor in Chennai, Mr. Bharathi said the DMK had already submitted a representation on July 4

title: Kerala PSC examination irregularities: Crime Branch team visits PSC headquarters
content: Investigators record detailed statement of complainants who secured the third and fifth ranks respectively in the recruitment process for Chief (Industry and Infrastructure) at State Planning Board

title: Street vendors urge MCC Commissioner to halt eviction drive till survey is completed
content: Citing the Street Vendors (Protection of Livelihood and Regulation of Street Vending) Act, a street vendors’ representative says the authorities cannot evict any street vendor until a survey is completed and ID cards and vending certificates are issued to the street vendors.

title: Tahir Hussain, four others convicted in IB staff Ankit Sharma murder case
content: Former Aam Aadmi Party councillor is convicted for murder, kidnapping with intent to confine, rioting with deadly weapons, among other serious charges, and acquitted on charges of hatching a criminal conspiracy and abetment of mutiny

title: Mysuru: State SVEEP Nodal Officer inspects Voter Facilitation Centre
content: During the visit, Mr. Vastrad reviewed the facilities provided to the public, the process of receiving applications, verification of supporting documents and the overall progress of the SIR exercise.

title: Three killed, 10 injured after truck rams into group of pilgrims in Pune
content: According to Pune Police, the truck was carrying warkaris from Nanded district, while the group came from Kasbedigraj in Sangli district

title: YSRCP announces Statewide protest for Rayalaseema Lift Irrigation Project completion
content: Alliance government stalled the the project despite payment of over ₹900 crore in bills and 80% completion of works during YSRCP regime, claims party’s State coordinator

title: Suspended BKTC employee arrested in Badrinath donation irregularities case
content: A four-member inquiry committee constituted by the Shri Badrinath-Kedarnath Temple Committee has found prima facie merit in allegations of irregularities in the management and counting of temple donations

title: SIT to revisit Ayodhya as probe expands in Ram temple funds embezzlement case
content: Probe team to investigate documents and question members of Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, says a senior Uttar Pradesh government official

title: Mysuru: Kannada activist Vatal Nagaraj threatens Karnataka bandh over eviction of footpath vendors
content: Mr. Nagaraj alleges that the government led by Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar had begun ‘oppressing the poor’ soon after assuming office by evicting street vendors across the State.

title: Kerala HC allows BJP councillor R. Sugathan to take oath in Viyyur jail
content: The Thiruvananthapuram Corporation councillor is under a six-month preventive detention under the Kerala Anti-Social Activities (Prevention) Act. He was among the 20 BJP councillors who had been directed by the High Court to retake their oaths following procedure violations. 19 councillors retook their oaths

title: Alampur Montessori School honours founder with ‘Sphoorthi 2026’ celebrations
content: Teachers and students pay homage by presenting speeches, poems, and songs highlighting various aspects of Kalyanamma’s inspiring life and her remarkable contribution to education

title: Four council meetings to set stage for no-confidence battle at Pala
content: The move has added a fresh political dimension to an already bitter power struggle between the Congress and the independent collective

title: After KOYO, Technopark-based HEX20 lines up six satellite missions, including MAYA-V1 and DINK-N1
content: Thiruvananthapuram-based space-tech start-up’s launches in the coming months include NILA III,  SAISI, COSPAR-1, and a lander module

title: Development of four lakes at a cost of ₹536 crore
content: The funds will be used to develop Fox Sagar (Jeedimetla), Ameenpur Lake, Amber Cheruvu (Nizampet), and Saroornagar Lake

title: Electricity store hamali union announce State-wide dharna on July 15
content: The union is seeking implementation of ‘equal pay for equal work,’ extension of EPF and ESI benefits, regularisation of contract employees, a minimum monthly wage of ₹48,000, free medical treatment through a health card, and insurance coverage of ₹1 crore for accidental death or injury

title: Expedite ropeway projects in 13 tourism destinations: Minister to officials
content: The feasibility studies for all 13 ropeway projects have been entrusted to Rail India Technical and Economic Service

title: Piduguralla Government Medical College gets NMC nod for 100 MBBS seats
content: College to admit its first batch of students from the 2026-27 academic year, strengthening medical education and healthcare in Palnadu district

title: Survey for Bidadi Township ends in chaos as women farmers chase officials with brooms
content: With this, the row over Bidadi township, championed by Chief Minister D. K. Shivakumar and has become a political flashpoint with Union Minister H.D. Kumaraswamy, has only escalated further.

title: Kerala court defers bail pleas of nine people in ED officials attack case
content: ED has strongly opposed the bail pleas and maintained that granting bail at the present stage could adversely affect the ongoing investigation

title: Ladakh to set up autonomous hill development councils for all seven districts: Chief Secretary
content: “Ladakh Administration has decided to constitute Autonomous Hill Development Council in each of the seven districts. It is a major step towards democratic decentralisation and grassroots governance,” Chief Secretary Ashish Kundra said

title: Omar Abdullah's "Hiding Behind Court" Jibe At BJP Over Rs 100 Crore Notice
content: The notice served by the BJP seeks an apology from Omar Abdullah or evidence to corroborate his allegation that the BJP offered Rs 20 to Rs 30 crore to National Conference legislators to switch sides.

title: 33,000 Assets, $407 Million Budget: Japan Builds Its First-Ever Spy Agency
content: At the heart of it all is Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, who sees a new domestic agency as central to her broader ambition of freeing Japan from the security constraints it has carried since the Second World War.

title: Man Behind Bangladesh's Tallest Ram Statue Arrested On Fraud Charges
content: Das, who had proposed building an 81-foot statue of Lord Ram at the Sri Sri Radha Gobinda Kali Temple in Palashbari in Gaibandha, has been arrested in a money laundering case involving suspicious financial transactions worth Tk9.35 crore.

title: Rs 1,160 Crore Rice 'Scam' Widens: 56 Mills, 22 Ethanol Plants Under Scanner
content: Investigators are trying to establish how much of the nearly 5 lakh metric tonnes of government rice allocated for ethanol production was actually converted into ethanol, and how much may have been diverted through private mills before returning to government warehouses.

title: Ex-AAP Leader Tahir Hussain Convicted For Intelligence Officer's Murder During Delhi Riots
content: The Karkardooma Court found Tahir Hussain, who was suspended by the AAP after his name came up in the case, guilty under the charges of promoting enmity, rioting, assault, criminal force and murder.

title: Trump's Iran War Closed Hormuz Strait. Now He Wants 'Fee' For Guarding It
content: Trump said the US had effectively been guarding the strait "for nothing" up to now, but that this would change, with wealthy nations expected to pay for the arrangement going forward.

title: PM Modi To Visit Punjab, Launch Rail Projects As BJP Gears Up For Solo Fight
content: PM Modi will inaugurate the redeveloped Jalandhar Cantonment Railway Station, a 110-year-old station refurbished by the Central government as per the Amrit Bharat Station Scheme.

title: A Truck, A Rice Mill And A Rs 1,160 Crore Question In Madhya Pradesh
content: The suspicion is that the government rice was diverted to private rice mills and later found its way back into government warehouses as custom milled rice.

title: UK Plans To Ban Iran's Elite Guards Over Threat To National Security
content: The banned groups would also include proxies and volunteers of Russia's GRU military intelligence agency, and the Islamic Movement of Companions of the Right (IMCR), an Iran-linked group which has claimed attacks on Jewish properties in London.

title: Aircraft Battling Colorado Wildfire Crashes, Pilot Dead
content: The pilot, believed to be the only person on board, was assisting firefighters with the 2-week-old Gold Mountain Fire

title: Agra Man Poisons Mother. His Mother-In-Law Had Told Him To Kill Her
content: Ravindra agreed to the condition, came back home, allegedly mixed poison into a chickpea dish and fed it to his mother, causing her condition to deteriorate.

title: Assam Rifles Jawan Killed In Action, 4 Injured In Suspected IED Blast In Nagaland
content: The suspected improvised explosive device (IED) detonated near an area where the Assam Rifles has a training centre

title: Man Fined Rs 1 Lakh For 3-Year-Old's Rape, Panchayat Used It For Liquor Party
content: Police said someone from the village had alerted them to what was happening. When officers arrived on Sunday, the celebration funded by the fine was underway.

title: 6-Year-Old Boy Sexually Assaulted By Attendant At Bengal School
content: The survivor's mother told the media that her son told her that the toilet attendant had subjected him to "oral molestation".

title: For 900 Years, It Was Believed To Be Saraswati Sculpture. Then A Revelation
content: The discovery has transformed a familiar museum object into one of the rarest known sculptural representations of Gayatri in India.

title: India's forex reserves explained: What they are, who owns them & how RBI uses them
content: the forex kitty had expanded to an all-time high of $728.494 billion during the week ended February 27 this year . the rupee came under pressure and the RBI had to intervene in the forex market through dollar sales . prime minister Narendra Modi has also appealed to countrymen to conserve forex by cutting down on foreign travel .

title: Beyond the 70% headline: H-1B workers becoming more educated, better paid
content: Lubna Kably is a senior editor, who focuses on various policies and legislation. In particular, she writes extensively on immigration and tax policies. The Indian diaspora is the largest in the world; through her articles she demystifies the immigration-policy related developments in select countries for outbound students, job aspirants and employees. She also analyses the impact of Income-tax and GST related developments for individuals and business entities. Read More
